report_name: Two-Thirds of US State Legislators Have Had Data Leaked on Dark Web

identities.name: U.S. State Legislators
identities.description: A class of government officials, 67% of whom have had their personal data, including plaintext passwords, exposed in third-party data breaches due to using official email addresses for personal services like LinkedIn, Adobe, and Dropbox.

identities.name: LinkedIn
identities.description: A professional networking service that was one of the sources of third-party data breaches exposing U.S. state legislators' data.

identities.name: Adobe
identities.description: A software company that was one of the sources of third-party data breaches exposing U.S. state legislators' data.

identities.name: Dropbox
identities.description: A file hosting service that was one of the sources of third-party data breaches exposing U.S. state legislators' data.

infrastructure.name: Dark Web Leak Sites
infrastructure.description: Used to circulate and sell breach compilations that contained the exposed data of U.S. state legislators.
